prompt: Why are tool-agent-user benchmarks important for evaluating deployed agents?
reference_answer: Tool-agent-user benchmarks are important because deployed agents must use tools while interacting with humans and following domain-specific policies. Tau-bench evaluates dynamic conversations between simulated users and tool-using agents, compares final database state to annotated goals, and uses pass^k to measure consistency across repeated trials. The broader lesson is that agent reliability depends on policy adherence, tool use, and user interaction together.

documents.title: tau-bench paper digest
documents.content: Tau-bench evaluates language agents in dynamic conversations with simulated users, domain-specific API tools, and policy guidelines. It highlights that real deployment requires agents to combine tool use, human interaction, and rule following.

documents.title: tau-bench evaluation digest
documents.content: Tau-bench evaluates final outcomes by comparing the database state at the end of a conversation against an annotated goal state. It also proposes pass^k to measure reliability over multiple trials.

documents.title: tau2-bench repository digest
documents.content: The tau2-bench repository extends tool-agent-user evaluation toward more realistic domains, including knowledge retrieval, voice, multimodal interaction, and task-quality fixes.

required_claims.text: Tool-agent-user benchmarks matter because deployed agents must combine tool use, user interaction, and policy adherence.
Tau-bench evaluates dynamic simulated-user conversations and checks final database state against annotated goals.
Reliability metrics such as pass^k are needed because agents can be inconsistent over repeated trials.

provenance.refresh_hint: Refresh if tau-bench or tau2-bench domain descriptions, evaluation metrics, or task quality notes change.
notes: Focused on deployed-agent evaluation with tools, users, and policies.
